channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil Tops $100 a Barrel as Attacks Spread Across Middle East
item.description: Global oil prices surged despite pledges from the United States and other countries to release reserves. Airstrikes shook Beirut and Tehran, and more ships were attacked in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s Retaliatory Strikes Appear to Be Slowing
item.description: U.S. officials say the country’s weapons have been diminished, slowing its attacks on Gulf nations and Israel. Iran may also be holding some weapons in reserve in case the conflict is prolonged.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Ephrat Livni and Riley Mellen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A missile was unveiled at a march to commemorate the 47th anniversary of the Islamic Revolution in Tehran last month, before the war.

item.title: How Trump’s War With Iran Changed the World in a Week
item.description: The conflict is reshaping travel patterns, energy dependencies, living costs, trade routes and diplomatic alliances.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising from oil storage in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: At China’s Big Political Meeting, a Rare Debate About Inequality
item.description: China’s plan to raise pensions for farmers by less than $3 a month prompted rare criticism from lawmakers about the country’s threadbare social safety net.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Andrea Verdelli for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Chinese government said it would increase the minimum basic pension for rural residents by 20 yuan a month, or about $3, to a grand total of 163 yuan a month.

item.title: Starmer Was Warned About Risk of Appointing Friend of Epstein As Ambassador
item.description: British officials told Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Peter Mandelson’s close ties to Jeffrey Epstein before Mr. Mandelson was appointed U.S. ambassador.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
item.description: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:credit: Nicole Tung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ukrainian drone operators in the Donetsk region in 2024. Drones now account for most Russian casualties, a Ukrainian commander said.

item.title: Why Did the UK Police Repeatedly Decline to Investigate Claims About Epstein and Prince Andrew?
item.description: The police in London interviewed Virginia Giuffre three times over her allegations about Jeffrey Epstein, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Ghislaine Maxwell, but never began a criminal investigation.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:credit: Joe Schildhorn/Patrick McMullan, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell in New York in 2005. Ms. Maxwell is serving a 20-year sentence for her role in Mr. Epstein’s sex-trafficking operation.

item.title: U.S. Seeks Extradition of Maduro Ally Alex Saab From Venezuela
item.description: The indicted tycoon Alex Saab is being detained in Venezuela on a U.S. request. His case tests President Trump’s sway over the country’s new rulers.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev, Annie Correal and Mariana Martínez
item.media:credit: Alejandro Cegarra for The New York Times
item.media:description: The businessman Alex Saab, center, in Caracas, Venezuela, in 2024.

item.title: A Trump-Xi Summit Nears, but China Doesn’t Know What Trump Wants
item.description: Trump’s agenda for the high-stakes meeting remains unclear to Beijing, Chinese analysts say, while American executives say they haven’t been invited along.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and David Pierson
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, meeting in Busan, South Korea, in October, where they announced a yearlong trade truce.

item.title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan
item.description: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: A photo released last year by Chinese state media showing aircraft on the Fujian, China’s third aircraft carrier.

item.title: Trump Casts a Shadow Over One of Mexico’s Deadliest States
item.description: President Trump wants to strike cartels inside Mexico. In Sinaloa State, a cartel stronghold, some residents said they were willing to entertain U.S. intervention.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas, Jack Nicas and Adriana Zehbrauskas
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: National Guard soldiers last month in Sinaloa, Mexico.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
item.description: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Russia Says Ukraine’s Strike on Factory City Was Deadly
item.description: Ukraine said the attack struck a key electronics plant, highlighting how it remains capable of hitting high-value targets inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.media:description: A still from a video released by the Ukraine armed forces of a strike on a Russian missile plant in Bryansk, Russia, on Tuesday.

item.title: A Dublin Pub-Crawl, but Hold the Booze
item.description: A wander through some famed watering holes in Ireland’s capital reveals a booming alcohol-free scene and a cultural shift.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia and Paulo Nunes Dos Santos
item.media:credit: Paulo Nunes dos Santos for The New York Times

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Elliott, John Yoon and Aurelien Breeden

item.title: This is what happened on March 11.
Haitian President’s Widow Recalls Killers Spoke Spanish and Used Nicknames
item.description: The prosecution used testimony by Martine Moïse about the language she heard on the night her husband was killed to support its claim that the assassination was carried out by a hired Colombian hit squad.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Martine Moïse, left, and her son in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, in 2021, after the attack that killed her husband.

item.title: Israel Bombards Lebanese Capital in Latest Round of Strikes
item.description: Lebanon’s health ministry said at least seven people were killed in the Beirut attacks early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Dayana Iwaza
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Dahiya, a densely populated residential area of southern Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday night.

item.title: U.S. to Release 172 Million Barrels of Oil From Strategic Reserves, Energy Dept. Says
item.description: The move aims to prevent prices from rising further because of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People filling up at a gas station in Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: U.N. Security Council Condemns Iran’s Retaliatory Strikes in the Middle East
item.description: In an overwhelming vote, the council backed a resolution condemning Iran. A Russian proposal calling for an end to the war that didn’t assign blame or even name the parties, was rejected.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United Nations Security Council during a meeting on Wednesday.

item.title: Countries Scramble to Shore Up Global Oil Supplies as Ships Are Attacked
item.description: The United States and Israel launched more strikes against Iran, where crowds mourned military commanders killed in the war. Israel also bombed targets in Lebanon, where the death toll climbed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Abdi Latif Dahir, Eric Schmitt and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A navy ship from the United Arab Emirates sailed next to a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.title: California Officials See No Imminent Threat of Drone Attacks by Iran
item.description: Just before the war began, the Federal Bureau of Investigation told state officials that Iran might respond with drone attacks on California. No specific, credible threats have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Laurel Rosenhall, Soumya Karlamangla and Shawn Hubler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California speaking at a news conference in Hayward, Calif., last week.

item.title: Two People Die After Paid Plasma Donation at Clinics in Canada
item.description: Grifols, a Spanish health care company, operates clinics in Canada that collect blood plasma from donors in exchange for an honorarium.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Roni Caryn Rabin
item.media:description: The plasma clinics were operated by Grifols, a Spanish health care company.

item.title: What Happened in the U.S.-Israeli War on Iran on Wednesday
item.description: Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah in Lebanon traded strikes on Wednesday. Three ships were hit near the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The aftermath of an Israeli airstrike on Wednesday in Ruwais, a neighborhood in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon.

item.title: Strong or Weak? How Trump Picks His Battles.
item.description: Despite his tough talk, President Trump has consistently made allowances for countries he sees as powerful or dominant.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia during a meeting in Alaska last summer. Mr. Trump’s deference to Russia highlights a pattern that has crystallized in recent months as he launches military action overseas.

item.title: Will Trump TACO on Iran?
item.description: It’s a crucial election year and economic and political costs of the war are rising. It won’t be easy for the president to declare victory and walk away.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and his aides have given conflicting indications on how long the U.S. intends the war to last.

item.title: Starmer Was Warned of Mandelson’s Ties to Epstein Ahead of Ambassador Pick
item.description: Documents released by the U.K. government on Wednesday showed that Prime Minister Keir Starmer was told of Peter Mandelson’s ties to the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson and Prime Minister Keir Starmer in Washington, in February last year.

item.title: First Week of Iran War Cost More Than $11 Billion, Pentagon Tells Congress
item.description: In a Capitol Hill briefing, officials gave their most comprehensive assessment of the cost of the first six days of the war, but the number omitted several aspects of the operation.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A closed-door briefing at the Capitol with Pentagon officials discussed the cost of the war.

item.title: Banks in Gulf Evacuate Their Offices
item.description: After an overnight attack on a bank, Iranian officials signaled a new willingness to target economic centers and banks with ties to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Citi, the global bank, told its staff to immediately depart what had been its longtime regional headquarters in the Dubai International Financial Center.

item.title: Lebanese residents are left in shock and fear as Israeli strikes reach the center of Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaayto and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:description: The recent Israeli strike killed four people according the Lebanese health ministry.

item.title: Iran Soccer Players Seeking Asylum Are Part of a History of Athlete Defections
item.description: Members of the Iranian soccer team who chose to remain in Australia this week are far from the first to travel to a competition and stay there.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Dave Hunt/Australian Associated Press, via Via Reuters
item.media:description: Several members of the Iranian women’s soccer team accepted asylum offers from Australia after participating in the AFC Women’s Asian Cup.

item.title: Iran holds a public mourning ceremony for commanders killed by strikes.
item.description: Thousands of people mourned in a state-sanctioned ceremony, as people in Tehran described deepening anxiety and fear as the war continued.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Sanam Mahoozi and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The procession was held after another night of intense strikes.

item.title: Israel sends an extra infantry brigade to Lebanon’s border.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Israeli artillery equipment deployed near the country’s border with Lebanon.

item.title: Iran Has Fired Widely Banned Cluster Munitions at Israel
item.description: Accounts from Israeli officials and footage verified by The New York Times show that Iran has targeted Israel with the weapons. Experts say this has exposed civilians to indiscriminate attacks.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Aaron Boxerman and Sanjana Varghese
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: See where U.S. installations have been damaged in the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Bora Erden and Leanne Abraham

item.title: U.S. at Fault in Strike on School in Iran, Preliminary Inquiry Says
item.description: Outdated targeting data may have resulted in a mistaken missile strike, according to the ongoing military investigation, which undercuts President Trump’s assertion that Iran could be to blame.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt, Tyler Pager, Malachy Browne and Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Why the British government is only releasing some of the Mandelson files.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson when he was Britain’s Ambassador to the United States last year, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Trump’s Actions in Iran and Venezuela Show Limits of U.S. Sanctions
item.description: America’s vast economic powers are able to wear down an adversary’s economy but are insufficient to topple leaders on their own.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Watching the aftermath of a strike in Tehran last week. Having reached the limits of its sanctions tools this year, the Trump administration is opting instead to use military force in places like Iran and Venezuela.

item.title: Who is Peter Mandelson?
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson was one of Britain’s most important political operatives for decades.

item.title: Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

item.title: At Least 3 Ships Are Struck Around Strait of Hormuz, and Iran Claims One
item.description: Three vessels were hit in and around the Persian Gulf on Wednesday, according to a British maritime agency. Iran claimed responsibility for one.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Royal Thai Navy, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An image released by the Royal Thai Navy shows a vessel near the Strait of Hormuz that was attacked on Wednesday. Iran claimed responsibility.

item.title: Iran to Skip World Cup, Minister Says, Despite Reported Trump Assurances to FIFA
item.description: The Iranian sports minister said taking part could not be countenanced. The comments came after the FIFA president, Gianni Infantino, said President Trump had told him Iran would be welcome.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iranian national soccer team before a match against Uzbekistan in March 2025. Iran qualified months ago for the World Cup, which will be held in the United States, Canada and Mexico this summer.

item.title: UK Bans Al Quds Protest March That Critics Say Supports Iranian Regime
item.description: Britain’s government invoked rarely-used powers to ban a pro-Palestinian protest march scheduled for Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle and Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Anti-war activists in London earlier this month.

item.title: Democrats Demand Accountability From Hegseth on Iranian School Strike
item.description: A majority of Senate Democrats called on Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth to carry out a “swift investigation” into the deadly attack on the first day of the war.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Protesters hold up photos of children killed by airstrikes on their school in Minab.

item.title: World Heritage Sites Hit in Airstrikes on Iran
item.description: Revered cultural icons that have withstood the upheavals of history are being hit hard in the war being waged by Israel and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Destruction of Cultural Antiquities and Heritage
United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Golestan Palace was damaged badly from an Israeli attack on a police station in Tehran last week.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Catie Edmondson, Abdi Latif Dahir, Rebecca Elliott and Liam Stack

item.title: This is what happened on March 10.
How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
item.description: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: In response to Iranian threats, commercial shipping has come to a standstill in the Gulf, and oil prices have spiked.

item.title: Missiles, Drones and Airstrikes Pound Middle East Into 12th Day of War
item.description: The effects of the war are being felt through the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An airstrike in Beirut’s southern suburbs late Tuesday night.

item.title: Widow Gives Harrowing Testimony About Assassination of Haiti’s President
item.description: Martine Moïse was the first witness called in the long-awaited Miami trial of the people accused of plotting the murder of her husband, Jovenel Moïse, in 2021.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Martine Moise, speaking during the funeral of her husband, Jovenel Moise, in Cap Haitien, Haiti, in 2021.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: Trump administration officials cast the president as the sole arbiter on the U.S. war effort. International aid groups were warning of a growing humanitarian crisis in Lebanon, where nearly 700,000 people had been displaced, the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Monday.

item.title: One Member of Iranian Soccer Squad Abandons Asylum Bid in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the Iranian women’s soccer delegation had initially opted to stay in Australia. The reversal was a sign of how fraught that decision was.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s soccer team arriving in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, after flying from Australia.

item.title: U.S. Forces Attack Iranian Mine-Laying Ships Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A video posted by the U.S. Central Command showed munitions striking nine vessels, most of which were moored at the time. Whether any mines have been laid in Gulf waters was unclear.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Boats docked in a fishing area in the Strait of Hormuz in Musandam, Oman, earlier this month.

item.title: Iranian Military Shows It Knows How to Adapt, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Iran appears to be targeting what it views as American vulnerabilities, including air defenses meant to guard troops and assets in the region.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran on Tuesday illustrating the succession of Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: Trump Tries to Sidestep Blame for Any Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: The president and the Pentagon have cast blame on Iran for the mounting toll. More than 1,800 people have died in the war, including many civilians.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has repeatedly suggested a strike on a girls’ school that reportedly killed 175 people was carried out by Iran or another country.

item.title: War in Iran Could Lead to Food Shortages in Region, Experts Warn
item.description: Some residents of Lebanon, Gaza and Iran are reporting shortages of food, rising food prices and other disruptions to food supplies as the conflict in the Middle East continues.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A food distribution center for internally displaced people in Dahiya, the densely populated southern suburb of Beirut that is a stronghold of Hezbollah, last week.

item.title: Boots on the Ground — but Whose?
item.description: The Kurds have been let down time and again by the U.S. Here’s why they might still partner with Americans in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
